title: Fermented Crispy Thai Fish Stir-fry
description: A sick-day favorite featuring fermented fish sauce and a crispy texture for added depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Fish fillets
ingredients.notes: Use white fish like cod or tilapia for a mild flavor.

ingredients.name: Fermented Fish Sauce
ingredients.notes: Ensure it's kosher-friendly as per instructions.

ingredients.name: Kaffir lime leaves
ingredients.notes: Fresh or dried, depending on availability.

ingredients.name: Chili flakes (optional)
ingredients.notes: Add a touch of heat if desired.

instructions: Preheat your wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Season the fish fillets with salt and pepper. If using, stir-fry the chili flakes briefly before removing them from the pan.
In the same pan, lightly toast sesame seeds until golden brown.
Add oil to the hot pan and swirl it around. When the oil is shimmering but not smoking, add garlic cloves and sauté for about 30 seconds or until fragrant.
Fold in fermented fish sauce evenly over the heated ingredients and stir-fry for another minute.

Gently place the fish fillets into the pan along with any accumulated juices from marinating or cooking. Cook for about 2-3 minutes per side, flipping gently to ensure even heat distribution and a crispy exterior.
Add kaffir lime leaves and continue stirring well until the fish is cooked through and evenly coated in the sauce mixture.
Remove from heat, garnish with extra sesame seeds if desired, and serve immediately.
